 29→/**

 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 61→                if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
 62→                    // Allow single line for:
 63→                    // else if (...)
 64→                    return
 65→                }
 66→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 68→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 70→                    return
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→
 74→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 75→            if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                autocorrect(node)
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 82→        val prevLeaves =
 83→            node
 84→                .leaves(forward = false)
 85→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 86→                .toList()
 87→                .reversed()
 88→        val nextLeaves =
 89→            node
 90→                .leaves(forward = true)
 91→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 92→                .toList()
 93→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 94→
 95→        prevLeaves
 96→            .firstOrNull()
 97→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 98→            ?.let {
 99→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
100→            }
101→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
102→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
103→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
104→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
105→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
106→            prevLeaves
107→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
108→                .forEach(::addChild)
109→            addChild(previousChild)
110→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
111→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
112→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
113→        }
114→
115→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
116→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
117→            node
118→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
120→        }
121→    }
122→}

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze the problem in the code:

In `MultiLineIfElseRule.kt`, the logic at lines 60-72 has a problem. When we have:
```kotlin
} else if (condition) {
```

The check at line 60 (`if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n'))`) will fail because there's a newline before `else`, so the early return for `else if` (lines 61-65) is never reached. This causes the rule to incorrectly wrap `else if` in braces.

The fix is to check for the `else if` pattern BEFORE checking for newlines, since `else if` should always be allowed regardless of whether the if-else statement is multiline.

The rule should NOT wrap `else if` when:- It's on the same line
- It already has proper blocks in its branches (which is the issue case)

 31→/**

 34→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 35→    StandardRule(
 36→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 37→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 38→            setOf(
 39→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 40→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 41→            ),
 42→    ) {
 43→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 44→
 45→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 46→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 47→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 48→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 49→        )
 50→    }
 51→
 52→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 53→        node: ASTNode,
 54→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 55→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 56→    ) {
 57→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 58→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 59→                return
 60→            }
 61→
 62→            // Check if this is an else if (either directly or within a continuation expression)
 63→            val ifNode = when (node.firstChildNode.elementType) {
 64→                IF -> node.firstChildNode
 65→                BINARY_EXPRESSION, DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION -> {
 66→                    // When there's a continuation operator like ?: or .plus() or .uppercase(),
 67→                    // the IF might be the first child of the continuation expression
 68→                    node.firstChildNode.firstChildNode?.takeIf { it.elementType == IF }
 69→                }
 70→                else -> null
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            if (ifNode != null) {
 74→                // Allow else if when it already has proper blocks in its branches
 75→                var thenNode: ASTNode? = null
 76→                var elseNode: ASTNode? = null
 77→
 78→                // Find THEN and ELSE children manually
 79→                var child = ifNode.firstChildNode
 80→                while (child != null) {
 81→                    when (child.elementType) {
 82→                        THEN -> thenNode = child
 83→                        ELSE -> elseNode = child
 84→                    }
 85→                    child = child.treeNext
 86→                }
 87→
 88→                val thenHasBlock = thenNode?.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK
 89→                val elseHasBlockOrIf = elseNode?.firstChildNode?.elementType.let { it == BLOCK || it == IF }
 90→
 91→                if (thenHasBlock && (elseNode == null || elseHasBlockOrIf)) {
 92→                    // The else if already has proper formatting with blocks, allow it
 93→                    return
 94→                }
 95→
 96→                // Otherwise check if it's a single line else if
 97→                if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
103→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
104→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
105→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
106→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
112→            if (autoCorrect) {
113→                autocorrect(node)
114→            }
115→        }
116→    }
117→
118→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
119→        val prevLeaves =
120→            node
121→                .leaves(forward = false)
122→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
123→                .toList()
124→                .reversed()
125→        val nextLeaves =
126→            node
127→                .leaves(forward = true)
128→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
129→                .toList()
130→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
131→
132→        prevLeaves
133→            .firstOrNull()
134→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
135→            ?.let {
136→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
137→            }
138→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
139→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
140→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
141→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
142→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
143→            prevLeaves
144→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
145→                .forEach(::addChild)
146→            addChild(previousChild)
147→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
148→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
149→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
150→        }
151→
152→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
153→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
154→            node
155→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
156→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
157→        }

## Summary of Changes

### Issue Description
The `multiline-if-else` rule was incorrectly reformatting `else if` statements when they had continuation operators (like `?: 0`, `.plus("foo")`, `.uppercase()`). It was wrapping the `else if` in braces, turning:
```kotlin
} else if (condition) {
    ...
}
```
into:
```kotlin
} else {
    if (condition) {
        ...
    }
}
```

### Root Cause
When there's a continuation operator on an if-else-if statement, the AST structure places the continuation expression (BINARY_EXPRESSION for `?:` or DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION for `.plus()`) as the first child of the ELSE node, with the IF as the first child of that continuation expression. The rule wasn't checking for this pattern and was incorrectly trying to wrap the entire continuation expression in braces.

### Solution
Modified the `MultiLineIfElseRule.kt` to:
1. Detect when an ELSE node's first child is a BINARY_EXPRESSION or DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION
2. Check if the first child of those expressions is an IF node
3. Verify that the IF has proper blocks in its branches
4. If properly formatted, allow it without emitting a violation

### Files Modified
1. `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRule.kt` - Added logic to handle continuation expressions

### Files Added
1. `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRuleElseIfContinuationTest.kt` - Test cases for the issue scenarios


I've implemented a targeted solution that preserves the intended code structure while preventing unintended reformatting of complex `else if` statements with continuation operators.

content.text:
Perfect! All tests pass successfully. Let me provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary of Changes

I've successfully fixed the false positive issue in the `multiline-if-else` rule when using `else if` with continuation operators.

### Problem
The rule was incorrectly reformatting `else if` statements that had continuation operators like `?: 0`, `.plus("foo")`, or `.uppercase()`. It was wrapping the `else if` in braces, turning:
```kotlin
} else if (condition) { ... }
```
into:
```kotlin
} else { if (condition) { ... } }
```

### Root Cause
When there's a continuation operator on an if-else-if statement, the AST structure places:
- A `BINARY_EXPRESSION` (for `?:`) or
- A `DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION` (for `.plus()`, `.uppercase()`, etc.)

as the first child of the ELSE node, with the IF as the first child of that expression. The rule wasn't checking for this pattern.

### Solution
Modified `MultiLineIfElseRule.kt` to:
1. Detect when an ELSE node's first child is a `BINARY_EXPRESSION` or `DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION`
2. Extract the IF node from within those expressions
3. Verify that the IF has proper blocks in its branches  
4. Allow it without emitting a violation if properly formatted

### Files Changed
1. **Modified**: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRule.kt`
   - Added imports for `BINARY_EXPRESSION` and `DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION`
   - Enhanced logic to handle continuation expressions (lines 62-100)

2. **Created**: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRuleElseIfContinuationTest.kt`
   - 5 comprehensive test cases covering the issue scenarios

### Verification
- ✅ All existing tests pass (22 tests in MultiLineIfElseRuleTest)
- ✅ All new tests pass (5 tests in MultiLineIfElseRuleElseIfContinuationTest)
- ✅ No regression in existing functionality

The fix ensures that `else if` statements with proper blocks and continuation operators are correctly preserved, while maintaining all existing rule behavior for other cases.

 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 61→                if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
 62→                    // Allow single line for:
 63→                    // else if (...)
 64→                    return
 65→                }
 66→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 68→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 70→                    return
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→
 74→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 75→            if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                autocorrect(node)
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 82→        val prevLeaves =
 83→            node
 84→                .leaves(forward = false)
 85→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 86→                .toList()
 87→                .reversed()
 88→        val nextLeaves =
 89→            node
 90→                .leaves(forward = true)
 91→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 92→                .toList()
 93→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 94→
 95→        prevLeaves
 96→            .firstOrNull()
 97→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 98→            ?.let {
 99→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
100→            }
101→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
102→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
103→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
104→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
105→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
106→            prevLeaves
107→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
108→                .forEach(::addChild)
109→            addChild(previousChild)
110→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
111→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
112→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
113→        }
114→
115→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
116→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
117→            node
118→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
120→        }
121→    }
122→}

 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :

 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(

 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }

 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
 61→                // Allow single line for:
 62→                // else if (...)
 63→                return
 64→            }
 65→

 67→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 68→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 70→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 71→                    return
 72→                }
 73→            }
 74→
 75→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 76→            if (autoCorrect) {

 78→                // Find THEN and ELSE children manually
 79→                var child = ifNode.firstChildNode
 80→                while (child != null) {
 81→                    when (child.elementType) {
 82→                        THEN -> thenNode = child
 83→                        ELSE -> elseNode = child
 84→                    }
 85→                    child = child.treeNext
 86→                }
 87→
 88→                val thenHasBlock = thenNode?.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK
 89→                val elseHasBlockOrIf = elseNode?.firstChildNode?.elementType.let { it == BLOCK || it == IF }
 90→
 91→                if (thenHasBlock && (elseNode == null || elseHasBlockOrIf)) {
 92→                    // The else if already has proper formatting with blocks, allow it
 93→                    return
 94→                }
 95→
 96→                // Otherwise check if it's a single line else if
 97→                if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
103→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
104→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
105→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
106→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
112→            if (autoCorrect) {
113→                autocorrect(node)
114→            }
115→        }
116→    }
117→
118→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
119→        val prevLeaves =
120→            node
121→                .leaves(forward = false)
122→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
123→                .toList()
124→                .reversed()
125→        val nextLeaves =
126→            node
127→                .leaves(forward = true)
128→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
129→                .toList()
130→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
131→
132→        prevLeaves
133→            .firstOrNull()
134→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
135→            ?.let {
136→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
137→            }
138→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
139→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
140→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
141→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
142→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
143→            prevLeaves
144→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
145→                .forEach(::addChild)
146→            addChild(previousChild)
147→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
148→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
149→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
150→        }
151→
152→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
153→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
154→            node
155→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
156→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
157→        }
158→    }
159→}
